flags.rewound_from_stage0: Case 2 (Bucket A) + regression pattern (a): same two flags recur across all 4 reviews, so the producer is bouncing off an upstream statement defect. Applying the (2a′) over-precision test to the producer's `regime_defining`-labelled [ass:sharp-dual-no-gap-endpoint-selectors]: the reviewer's defect is endpoint ATTAINMENT + measurable selectors + dual no-gap for a strict-indicator (lsc) kernel — a regularity artifact of an OVER-STATED focal object (a closed sharp interval with attained endpoints and an asserted primal=dual EQUALITY), not a restriction on the population of laws. Every review explicitly offers the standard alternative ('outer bound / closure-sharp interval', 'weaken to the primal envelope'). This fails to be genuinely regime_defining; it is over-precision → statement_correction (NOT a theorem_split, which the producer's misclassification would have forced). (2a′) gates all hold: same object (the identified set / conditional value, restated as its standard closure / primal form), unconditional (the outer bound Θ_I⊆[L,U] and primal inf/sup are reproduced from the definitions under the existing Assumptions sampling–band, no new hypothesis), novelty-preserving (the copula-band primal characterization still differs from the copula-free / fixed-dependence / non-causal §4 comparators), no laundering (only the extremal/closure form is restated, no comparator estimand assumed). The second proposed premise [ass:root-n-local-sieve] for Theorem 2 is a genuine root-n stochastic-equicontinuity/sieve-remainder regularity (caveat), needs the existing producer caveat-grant hook (it is NOT a pure restatement and fails gate (ii)), so it is handled by the producer's own caveat path on the same Stage-0 rewind; this decision targets the dominant, mis-classified flagship defect.

proposed_from.topic: Continuous-outcome nonparametric sharp bounds on the proportion who benefit from treatment, Pr(Y1 > Y0), under a rank-concordance band — Spearman's rho of the potential-outcome copula constrained to [rho_minus, rho_plus] rather than a fixed copula family — with the critical concordance rho* at which the benefit-majority sign (Pr > 1/2 vs < 1/2) becomes data-undetermined; plug-in endpoint estimators and Imbens-Manski/Stoye inference over the partially-identified interval. Differentiate from arXiv:2605.11415 (ordinal latent-threshold, single-family envelope) and Fan-Park Makarov (marginals-only, copula free).
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue ancestor is pid_gpn_continuous_rho, which introduced fixed-rho continuous GPN envelope notation and inverse rho-star language but left sharpness and measurable pasting unresolved for a different event. The current proposal targets Pr(Y1>Y0), replaces the old attained-endpoint claim with a closure-sharp value set and weak-duality envelope, and records the no-gap Spearman-band transport step as the open conjecture. The failed pid_bound_intersection_inference scaffold motivates the root-n local sieve condition rather than a generic bootstrap assertion.","published_axis":"FanPark2009DTEConfidenceSets treats known dependence information, FanPark2010DTEBounds treats copula-free Makarov endpoints, Liang2026RankStickiness gives rank-stickiness point identification, and HeLi2026OrdinalCopula fixes a parametric ordinal copula. None states the causal closure envelope for the strict benefit event over all continuous-outcome copulas satisfying a Spearman band, nor the associated no-gap duality conjecture, outer-envelope majority diagnostic, and finite-cell plug-in endpoint inference route."}
seed_list: Derive a sharp primal-dual optimal-transport formula for Pr(Y1>Y0) over all continuous-outcome copulas whose Spearman rho lies in [rho_minus,rho_plus], and pair it with plug-in endpoint inference.
Characterize the critical concordance set R_{1/2}(P) at which the majority-benefit sign is data-undetermined, with scalar rho* thresholds under a single-crossing benefit boundary.
Construct quantile-grid plug-in estimators for the Spearman-band benefit endpoints and prove an m-out-of-n bootstrap law feeding Imbens-Manski/Stoye confidence intervals.
Compare unconditional pooled, integrated conditional, and globally budgeted Spearman-band benefit bounds, proving when covariate-cell rho budgets can and cannot be safely pooled.
Prove or refute measurable extremal-copula pasting for conditional continuous marginals under one global Spearman rank-moment budget.
Define the correct Spearman-band benefit functional with atoms and ties, and determine whether ordinal latent-threshold copula envelopes converge to the continuous-outcome rho-band bounds.
Give necessary and sufficient conditions under which every copula in a Spearman band implies the same majority-benefit sign for Pr(Y1>Y0).
Develop a finite rank-copula LP with explicit approximation error certificates for the continuous Spearman-band benefit endpoint and its active rank-moment constraints.
Build a calibrated projection confidence set for the inverse rho* majority threshold by adapting intersection-bound precision correction to estimated benefit endpoint curves.
Intersect mutual stochastic-increasing restrictions with a Spearman rho band and characterize the resulting sharp benefit interval as a nonparametric dependence-restricted coupling problem.

seed_details.one_line: Derive a sharp primal-dual optimal-transport formula for Pr(Y1>Y0) over all continuous-outcome copulas whose Spearman rho lies in [rho_minus,rho_plus], and pair it with plug-in endpoint inference.

anchor_paper.author: Fan and Park
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp Makarov treatment-effect distribution bounds with no dependence information; conclusion says dependence information could tighten them.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Fan-Park Section 6: incorporate prior information on dependence measures of potential outcomes.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-cluster LP risk avoided: use copula/transport duality, not a generic finite LP.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Fan-Park plus a moment constraint because it is just Makarov with one more linear restriction.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the objective is the benefit half-plane and the global Spearman moment changes the extremal coupling and sharpness/pasting argument.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the critical concordance set R_{1/2}(P) at which the majority-benefit sign is data-undetermined, with scalar rho* thresholds under a single-crossing benefit boundary.

anchor_paper.author: Mullahy
anchor_paper.claim: Inequality probabilities such as one potential outcome exceeding another are policy-relevant and boundable from marginals.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Mullahy motivates benefit-majority signs but does not give a rank-concordance threshold.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Critical-delta shape is stronger because rho* is the named scalar/set where the sign changes.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from plotting L(rho) and U(rho) because any threshold is definitional.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the nontrivial work is proving endpoint curve orientation or nonmonotone critical-set structure under a rank-moment constraint.

seed_details.one_line: Construct quantile-grid plug-in estimators for the Spearman-band benefit endpoints and prove an m-out-of-n bootstrap law feeding Imbens-Manski/Stoye confidence intervals.

anchor_paper.author: Fan and Park
anchor_paper.claim: They derive nonparametric endpoint estimators and compare asymptotic, n-bootstrap, and fewer-than-n bootstrap inference for Makarov bounds.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Fan-Park Section 6 points from endpoint inference to confidence sets for identified sets and nonregular problems.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M8 estimator rung is nonroutine because active rank-moment faces replace closed-form Makarov extrema.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Fan-Park bootstrap and Imbens-Manski interval formulas.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Imbens-Manski/Stoye need endpoint limit theory, and Fan-Park does not cover empirical infinite-dimensional transport with a binding Spearman moment.

seed_details.one_line: Compare unconditional pooled, integrated conditional, and globally budgeted Spearman-band benefit bounds, proving when covariate-cell rho budgets can and cannot be safely pooled.

anchor_paper.author: Fan, Guerre, and Zhu
anchor_paper.claim: They characterize identified sets for joint-potential-outcome functionals and show covariates can shrink sets.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Their covariate logic leaves open whether a global concordance budget decomposes by covariate cell.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: T=pooled-to-conditional extension is stronger because global rho can fail to allocate additively across cells.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from conditioning on X and integrating conditional endpoints.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: a global Spearman moment is one aggregate budget; optimizing cellwise can violate or underuse the shared budget.

seed_details.one_line: Prove or refute measurable extremal-copula pasting for conditional continuous marginals under one global Spearman rank-moment budget.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Prior proposal explicitly stated that sharpness needs a measurable conditional pasting argument.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Sharpness needs a measurable conditional pasting argument

seed_details.anchor_followup: Prior proposal Section 10/11 left measurable conditional pasting as the sharpness obstacle.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/sharpness-failure is stronger because it targets a named failed CausalSmith proof dependency rather than a self-invented shortcut.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a technical lemma inside the rho-band endpoint proof.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: without pasting, local endpoint values may not be sharp for any global latent law; prior reviewers flagged this exact issue.

seed_details.one_line: Define the correct Spearman-band benefit functional with atoms and ties, and determine whether ordinal latent-threshold copula envelopes converge to the continuous-outcome rho-band bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Zhang and Richardson
anchor_paper.claim: They show discontinuous marginals create sharpness and logical-gap issues in Makarov treatment-effect applications.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Their discontinuity audit implies Spearman/tie conventions must be settled before ordinal approximations can stand in for continuous bounds.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Ordinal-to-continuous extension is stronger because atoms make Spearman copulas nonunique rather than merely discretizing a smooth problem.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is solved by finite ordinal LP bounds such as He-Li or Huang et al.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: finite ordinal latent-threshold copulas and continuous rank-moment copulas are different objects when ties make copulas nonunique.

seed_details.one_line: Give necessary and sufficient conditions under which every copula in a Spearman band implies the same majority-benefit sign for Pr(Y1>Y0).

anchor_paper.author: Firpo and Ridder
anchor_paper.claim: They show pointwise Makarov bounds are not uniformly sharp for functionals.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Their direct-functional lesson suggests majority signs need a direct iff condition, not CDF-point inversion.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sufficiency-trap avoided by asking for necessity and sufficiency of sign determination over the whole band.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate because sign determination is just L>1/2 or U<1/2.
seed_details.rebuttal: Partly true for a fixed band; the net-new content is a primitive condition on marginals/rho under which the iff set has a computable form.

seed_details.one_line: Develop a finite rank-copula LP with explicit approximation error certificates for the continuous Spearman-band benefit endpoint and its active rank-moment constraints.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Finite rank-copula LP templates used critical-rho phase language and endpoint-attaining LP constructions.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: lower and upper endpoints ... are attained by linear programs over this polytope

seed_details.anchor_followup: Prior LP template suggests a rank-grid approximation, but not continuous benefit endpoints or transport error certificates.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M4 default risk acknowledged; made stronger by explicit approximation-error certificate and active-constraint witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine discretization of a continuous transport problem.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the rank-moment and discontinuous benefit indicator require a certified checkerboard error bound to avoid nonsharp finite-grid artifacts.

seed_details.one_line: Build a calibrated projection confidence set for the inverse rho* majority threshold by adapting intersection-bound precision correction to estimated benefit endpoint curves.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Lee, and Rosen
anchor_paper.claim: Intersection-bound inference supplies precision correction for extrema of estimated bound functions.

seed_details.anchor_followup: The rho* object is an inverse crossing of estimated endpoint curves, not a scalar interval endpoint.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Critical-delta/inference shape is stronger because rho* is an inverse crossing with precision correction, not generic CI inversion.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Chernozhukov-Lee-Rosen intersection bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the estimated objects are sharp transport endpoint curves with active-set changes and an inverse majority-sign crossing.

seed_details.one_line: Intersect mutual stochastic-increasing restrictions with a Spearman rho band and characterize the resulting sharp benefit interval as a nonparametric dependence-restricted coupling problem.

anchor_paper.author: Frandsen and Lefgren
anchor_paper.claim: They impose testable mutual stochastic increasingness to sharpen DTE bounds beyond classical inequalities.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Their dependence restrictions suggest combining shape restrictions with a scalar rank-concordance budget.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption-combination risk is controlled by asking for the sharp intersection, not a sufficient-only tightened interval.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate by intersecting their feasible set with the Spearman moment constraint.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: MSI is an order restriction while Spearman is a global integral moment; their intersection needs new extremal-coupling characterization.

proposed_from.literature_map:
1. Fan-Park 2010 provide the continuous-outcome Makarov benchmark and endpoint inference, but their conclusion leaves dependence-information tightening open.
2. Zhang-Richardson 2025 shows Kolmogorov/Makarov sharpness and achievability require care, especially around discontinuities.
3. Firpo-Ridder 2019 shows pointwise treatment-effect CDF bounds do not automatically give sharp functional bounds.
4. Mullahy 2018 motivates inequality probabilities such as Pr(Y1>Y0) as applied health-evaluation parameters.
5. He-Li 2026 gives the closest copula-sensitivity arc for probability of benefit, but fixes a parametric copula and works with ordinal outcomes.
6. Fan-Guerre-Zhu 2017 and Frandsen-Lefgren 2021 show covariates and nonparametric dependence restrictions can tighten joint-potential-outcome bounds.
7. Kellerer 1984 supplies the transport-duality template for coupling optimization with marginal constraints.
8. Imbens-Manski 2004 and Stoye 2009 supply the interval-ID inference target once endpoint limit theory exists.
9. Chernozhukov-Lee-Rosen 2013 and Kaido-Molinari-Stoye 2019 are adjacent for inverse crossing/projection inference.
10. No located paper gives sharp continuous-outcome benefit bounds over all copulas satisfying a Spearman rho band.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Endpoint attainment, measurable optimizing selectors, and primal-dual equality are not claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Theorem 1 is bound to the closure-sharp integrated feasible value set plus weak-duality outer bounds; no-gap equality is only Conjecture 1.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Envelope straddling of 1/2 is not claimed to construct feasible laws on both sides of the majority boundary.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Theorem 2 is accepted only as an outer-envelope diagnostic: certification outside the envelope, indeterminacy at the outer-envelope level when it straddles.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Endpoint inference is conditional on stable dual faces and the stated root-n local sieve-remainder condition.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The bootstrap endpoint-law claim is a conditional field-tier inference conjecture, not an unconditional endpoint theorem.

banked_reason: D0.5.G cold referee: delivered an exact dual representation + delta-method/bootstrap inference for an OUTER Spearman-band endpoint under strong regularity, not the sharp identified-set equality / sign-indeterminacy result the headline promised — a sound subfield partial-ID contribution, below the field target.
